thesis: Nokia is the #2 global telecom equipment vendor with a durable patent licensing business and improving optical/network infrastructure exposure via the Infinera acquisition. Mobile Networks margin pressure and 5G capex maturation create near-term headwinds, but the dividend yield (~3.5%), net cash position (€3.8B), and AI-driven fiber demand provide a floor. HOLD pending a clearer mobile networks recovery or a pullback to more attractive entry levels.

financials.fcf: Positive; dividend and buyback supported by strong balance sheet

drivers: Mobile Networks margins recover to 8–10% as pricing stabilizes and cost actions take hold
AI-driven optical/fiber demand accelerates post-Infinera integration, expanding data center interconnect revenue
Major patent licensing renewals exceed expectations, boosting Nokia Technologies revenue and margins
Government broadband stimulus (BEAD, EU digital sovereignty) drives fixed access and fiber sales
Private wireless enterprise segment scales faster than expected, diversifying revenue away from cyclical RAN capex

assumptions: Modest mobile networks recovery with operating margins stabilizing near 5–7%
Network Infrastructure grows mid-single digits on optical and IP routing demand
Nokia Technologies licensing revenue flat-to-up on renewals at similar terms
FX and geopolitical headwinds remain manageable
Valuation multiple holds at ~18x forward P/E

risks: Mobile Networks margin pressure persists due to aggressive competition from Ericsson and Samsung
Geopolitical disruptions (India tariffs, China restrictions) limit market access and increase costs
Patent licensing disputes or downward rate pressure reduce Nokia Technologies revenue
5G capex cycle slows before 5G-Advanced ramps, creating a revenue and earnings gap
Open RAN adoption accelerates, eroding incumbent RAN vendor pricing power

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 earnings — watch Mobile Networks margin trajectory and Infinera integration progress
Potential announcement of major patent licensing renewals
Capital Markets Day / 2027 guidance — management may refine margin and growth targets
U.S. BEAD broadband awards and European digital sovereignty programs favor Nokia fiber access

key_risks: Mobile Networks margin compression from intense RAN competition
Geopolitical headwinds (India tariffs, China market access restrictions)
FX volatility (EUR/USD) impacting reported euro-denominated results
5G capex cycle maturation in developed markets before 5G-Advanced upgrade cycle begins
Open RAN and network virtualization trends eroding incumbent vendor margins over time
